steps.title: Read the original radius
steps.content:
A circle in standard form has equation $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$. For circle A, the center is $(3,9)$ and
$$r^2=25$$
$$r=5$$

steps.title: Double and square the radius
steps.content:
Circle B has the same center, but its radius is twice as large.
$$2(5)=10$$
The equation must use the square of that radius.
$$10^2=100$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content:
Graphing the original circle and the circle with doubled radius shows the same center and the larger radius. Keep the same center and replace the right side with $100$.
$$(x-3)^2+(y-9)^2=100$$
The answer is D.
